maxContractTerm: **Three (3) years maximum, with mutual 90-day termination right by either party.** Iowa Code § 285.5(1)(a) (2026) provides verbatim (re-opened on legis.iowa.gov, Iowa Code 2026): "Contracts for school bus service with private parties shall be in writing and be for the transportation of children who attend public school and children who attend nonpublic school. ... Contracts may be made for a period not to exceed three years." Iowa Code § 285.5(4) provides verbatim: "All contracts may be terminated by either party on a ninety-day notice." The Iowa Department of Education's prescribed uniform contract form (TR-F-4-497 (Rev. 12/03), "CONTRACT for PUBLIC STUDENT TRANSPORTATION with a Contractor"), which Iowa Code § 285.5(5) requires "shall be used by all schools in contracting for transportation service," contains a bracketed instruction on the term line stating "[Period not to exceed 3 years.]" and a "Miscellaneous" clause stating: "(A) This contract may not be terminated prior to the conclusion of a school semester unless otherwise agreed upon by both parties. A 90-day written termination notice shall be given." The functional cap is therefore best stated as "3 years with any-time termination permitted on 90-day written notice (subject to a semester-end limitation absent mutual agreement)." Cite: Iowa Code § 285.5(1)(a), (4), (5) (https://www.legis.iowa.gov/docs/code/285.5.pdf); IDOE Form TR-F-4-497 (https://educate.iowa.gov/media/2087/download?inline).
rebidRequired: **Not required by state statute.** No Iowa statute or regulation requires a public school district to conduct a competitive process (sealed bid, RFP, RFQ, or other statutorily-mandated competitive selection) when a § 285.5 private-contractor transportation contract reaches the end of its term. Iowa Code § 285.5 is silent on procurement method; it specifies form (written), required content, the three-year cap, the 90-day termination right, mandatory DOE uniform form, insurance, driver approval, and other operational requirements, but imposes no bidding obligation for the award or renewal of the service contract itself. Iowa Code § 285.10(3) authorizes local boards "to enter into contracts for transportation subject to any provisions of law affecting same," and the only "provisions of law affecting same" specific to contractor selection are § 285.5 and IAC 281—43.21 — neither of which prescribes a bidding requirement for the service contract. Iowa's competitive bidding regime in Iowa Code ch. 26 and § 73A.18 reaches only "construction, erection, demolition, alteration or repair of a public improvement" (verbatim from § 73A.18 (2026)), not operating service contracts. Iowa Code ch. 297 (school district property/contracts) governs schoolhouse construction and real-property dealings, not operating service procurement. Iowa law does not set a statutory dollar threshold above which a school district must competitively bid operating goods or services; that decision rests with the local board, as reflected in district policy 705.1 / 705.01 templates issued through Iowa school-finance policy services. The only statutorily mandated rebid event in §§ 285.1–285.16 is the obligation that the contract simply terminate at the end of its stated term (no more than three years) — so to continue services beyond the term, the board must enter a new written contract (which it may award by any lawful method, including direct re-engagement of the incumbent or competitive solicitation, at board discretion). Cite: Iowa Code § 285.5 (https://www.legis.iowa.gov/docs/code/285.5.pdf); Iowa Code ch. 285 (https://www.legis.iowa.gov/docs/ico/chapter/285.pdf); Iowa Code § 285.10(3); Iowa Code § 73A.18 (https://www.legis.iowa.gov/docs/code/73A.18.pdf); IDOE Bureau of School Improvement Services, "Management of Ancillary Services — Transportation," Chapter 14 (https://educate.iowa.gov/media/3274/download?inline=).
renewalMechanics: **No statutory renewal mechanism; expiration requires a new written contract subject to the same 3-year cap.** Iowa Code § 285.5 contains no provision authorizing an automatic rollover, evergreen continuation, statutory CPI/index adjustment, fuel escalator, mutual-written-agreement extension beyond the cap, agency approval renewal, or board-approval extension. Because the statute caps any single contract at three years and is silent on extensions or option years, the operative practice is to enter a new written § 285.5 contract on the DOE's uniform form, again subject to the same three-year ceiling and the same § 285.5(4) 90-day termination right. The contract's economic terms — compensation, fuel/index adjusters, escalators, route-change pricing, insurance, and operational covenants — are negotiated within the document (see, e.g., the prescribed IDOE form fields for "compensation" under § 285.5(1)(a), and the Iowa Administrative Code's mandatory contract provision IAC 281—43.21(14) (adopted by ARC 7793C, IAB 4/17/24, eff. 5/22/24) requiring the contract "[t]o reserve the right of the board of education to change routing of the bus and, if additional mileage is required, allow for an extra cost"). The board has discretion to negotiate any in-term price-adjustment or pass-through mechanism that does not conflict with chapter 285, and § 285.5(1)(b) prescribes a mandatory contractor-to-board equipment-purchase mechanism on contractor-initiated termination (IAC 281—43.21(17) implements this, expressly exempting passenger vehicles used as school buses). Beyond these in-term mechanics, no statutory or regulatory "renewal without rebid" mechanism exists; the statute simply caps each successive instrument at three years. **N/A** for automatic rollover/evergreen, statutory CPI auto-escalator, statutory option-year extension, or agency-approval renewal — none of these exist in Iowa law for § 285.5 contracts. Cite: Iowa Code § 285.5(1)(a), (1)(b), (4), (5) (https://www.legis.iowa.gov/docs/code/285.5.pdf); IAC 281—43.21 (current version adopted ARC 7793C, IAB 4/17/24, eff. 5/22/24, with one subrule amended by ARC 8240C, IAB 10/2/24, eff. 11/6/24) (https://www.legis.iowa.gov/docs/ACO/rule/281.43.21.pdf); IDOE Form TR-F-4-497 (https://educate.iowa.gov/media/2087/download?inline).
statutoryCitation: **Primary statute:** Iowa Code § 285.5 (2026), "Contracts for transportation," subsections (1)(a) (writing, content, **three-year cap**), (1)(b) (equipment buy-out on contractor termination), (2) (driver approval), (3) (vehicle inspection/approval), (4) (**90-day mutual termination right**), (5) (mandatory DOE uniform contract form), (6) (insurance), (7) (board-outside contracting rule), (8) (private-bus standards), (9) (driver contracts for school-owned buses), official URL: https://www.legis.iowa.gov/docs/code/285.5.pdf. **Containing chapter:** Iowa Code ch. 285, "State Aid for Transportation" (Iowa Code 2026), https://www.legis.iowa.gov/docs/ico/chapter/285.pdf. **Cross-referenced statutes establishing board authority and applicability:** Iowa Code § 285.10(1)–(3) (duty to provide transportation; authority "to enter into contracts for transportation subject to any provisions of law affecting same"); Iowa Code § 285.1(17)(b) (express authorization to satisfy transportation duty by "[c]ontracting with private parties as provided in section 285.5"); Iowa Code § 285.2(4)(b) (district may contract "with a private contractor under section 285.5" for contiguous-district transport); Iowa Code § 285.8 (DOE supervisory authority and rule-making source for IAC 281 ch. 43); Iowa Code § 285.11 (operational considerations for routes and contracting, including subsection 4 special-contract authority for individual pupils for whom regular-route transport is uneconomical or unsafe due to disability). **Implementing administrative regulations (current effective text):** Iowa Administrative Code 281—Chapter 43, "Pupil Transportation" (Division II "Private Contractors": Rules 281—43.3 through 281—43.7; Division VII Rule 281—43.21 "Contract — privately owned buses" requiring all 17 enumerated provisions, including subrule 43.21(14) (board's right to change bus routing with mileage cost allowance), subrule 43.21(16) (the § 285.5(4) 90-day termination requirement), and subrule 43.21(17) (the § 285.5(1) equipment-buy-out provision)). The current chapter 43 was adopted by ARC 7793C (IAB 4/17/24, eff. 5/22/24), with one rule (43.13) further amended by ARC 8240C (IAB 10/2/24, eff. 11/6/24); chapter text at https://www.legis.iowa.gov/docs/iac/chapter/281.43.pdf and rule text at https://www.legis.iowa.gov/docs/ACO/rule/281.43.21.pdf. The proposing notice for the 2024 rewrite is ARC 7590C, IAB 2/7/24 (https://www.legis.iowa.gov/docs/aco/arc/7590C.pdf), with legal authority cited as Iowa Code § 285.8 and implementing Iowa Code ch. 285. **Code currency:** As of 2026-06-23, the Iowa Code 2026 PDFs on legis.iowa.gov reflect § 285.5 as last amended by 2010 Iowa Acts ch. 1061, § 180, and 2011 Iowa Acts ch. 25, § 24 (amendment history line on the § 285.5 PDF reads "85 Acts, ch 212, §21; 2010 Acts, ch 1061, §180; 2011 Acts, ch 25, §24"). No substantive amendment to § 285.5 was enacted in the 2012–2025 sessions; the 2023 Acts ch. 19, § 1052 amendment that appears in the chapter shows up in § 285.1's history line only.
districtVariance: **No (with caveat that boards retain operational discretion within statutory floors).** As to the binding rules of § 285.5 — three-year cap, 90-day termination, mandatory IDOE-prescribed uniform contract form (Form TR-F-4-497), and the seventeen content provisions enumerated in IAC 281—43.21 — Iowa applies these uniformly statewide. Iowa Code § 285.5(5) makes the IDOE uniform contract form mandatory ("shall be used by all schools in contracting for transportation service"), and the State Board's IAC 281—43.3 (adopted by ARC 7793C, eff. 5/22/24) makes the contract requirement universal: "All private contractors wishing to transport pupils to and from school in privately owned vehicles are to be under contract with the local board of education." No credible evidence of district-by-district variance in the interpretation of the three-year cap or the 90-day termination right was located after reviewing: (i) the IDOE's authoritative manual "Management of Ancillary Services — Transportation" (Chapter 14), which paraphrases § 285.5 verbatim for all districts; (ii) IDOE Pupil Transportation guidance at https://educate.iowa.gov/pk-12/operation-support/pupil-transportation; (iii) Iowa Association of School Boards 2024 legislative summary; (iv) two representative district purchasing/bidding policies re-verified during validation (Iowa City CSD policy 705.1 and South Tama County CSD policy 705.01) — neither displaces or reinterprets § 285.5. The only meaningful variance across districts is in matters Iowa law expressly leaves to local discretion — e.g., the dollar threshold at which a district will solicit competitive quotations or bids for operating goods and services; the choice of in-term price-adjustment mechanism written into the negotiated contract; insurance limits set under § 285.5(6) and IAC 281—43.21(10); and the practical question of whether to re-solicit competitively at term-end versus negotiate a successor contract with the incumbent. These are local-policy variances within a uniform statutory ceiling, not interpretive variance over the statute itself.
specialEducation: **Iowa law does not set a different contract term, rebid requirement, or renewal mechanic for special-education student transportation; the same § 285.5 / IAC 281—43.21 framework controls.** Iowa Code ch. 285 — particularly § 285.5 (contractor terms), § 285.10 (board duties and authority to "enter into contracts for transportation"), and § 285.11(4) ("Special contracts for transportation of pupils entitled to transportation shall be entered into only when it is more economical to make such special provision than to provide same by regular bus route, or when by reason of physical or mental disability of the pupil such pupil cannot be transported with safety by bus") — applies to all student transportation, including transportation of students with disabilities, IEP-mandated transportation, and McKinney-Vento students. The phrase "Special contracts" in § 285.11(4) refers to individualized transportation arrangements (e.g., a student-specific arrangement for a child with a disability that cannot ride a regular bus), not a separate procurement statute; such arrangements still flow through § 285.5 when entered with a private contractor. Iowa Code ch. 256B (Special Education) and the implementing rules in IAC 281—Chapter 41 (Special Education) govern the substantive entitlement to transportation as a "related service" (mirroring IDEA, 34 C.F.R. § 300.34(c)(16)). The specific Iowa special-education transportation rule is **IAC 281—41.412 "Transportation"** (re-verified during validation), which sets substantive requirements (eligibility, equipment standards conforming to 281—chs. 43 and 44, AEA/LEA cost allocation, IEP specifications) but expressly does not establish any separate contract-term ceiling, rebid trigger, or renewal mechanic for the contracting relationship with a private vendor — those continue to be governed by § 285.5 and IAC 281 ch. 43. (Note: Stage 1 erroneously cited IAC 281—41.530(5); the correct cite is IAC 281—41.412.) The IDOE Chapter 14 manual confirms that AEAs may participate in providing special-education transportation under Iowa Code § 256B.4(1)(e) ("If special education services are provided by the AEA, the board of directors of the AEA with the cooperation of the local school districts within its jurisdiction may provide transportation for children requiring special education services that are in need of transportation in connection with any programs, classes, or services"), but the resulting contract with a private contractor remains subject to § 285.5 in form and term. **Federal IDEA mandate:** IDEA's requirement that schools provide transportation as a related service to eligible children (34 C.F.R. § 300.34(c)(16)) is a duty-to-transport federal mandate, not a state contracting rule, and it does not displace or alter Iowa's § 285.5 contract-term cap or 90-day termination right. **McKinney-Vento / foster care / homeless transportation:** Iowa has not enacted special procurement rules for these populations; transportation provided through a private contractor for these students still uses a § 285.5 contract. **Conclusion:** No separate contract-term ceiling, no separate rebid requirement, and no separate renewal mechanic exists in Iowa law for special-education student transportation; § 285.5 (3 years max; 90-day termination; uniform DOE form) governs uniformly. Cite: Iowa Code §§ 285.5, 285.10, 285.11(4), 256B.4(1)(e); IAC 281—43.3; IAC 281—41.412; IDOE Manual ch. 14 ("Management of Ancillary Services — Transportation"), https://educate.iowa.gov/media/3274/download?inline=.
notesAndSpecialProvisions:
- **Mandatory DOE-prescribed uniform contract.** Iowa Code § 285.5(5) commands: "The director of the department of education shall prepare a uniform contract containing provisions not in conflict with this chapter which shall be used by all schools in contracting for transportation service." The current form is IDOE Form TR-F-4-497 (Rev. 12/03), "CONTRACT for PUBLIC STUDENT TRANSPORTATION with a Contractor" (https://educate.iowa.gov/media/2087/download?inline). The contract form's term line carries a bracketed limit "[Period not to exceed 3 years.]" Districts may add "Additional Provisions" but cannot displace the chapter-285 requirements.
- **Seventeen mandatory contract content provisions (IAC 281—43.21).** The current Iowa Administrative Code (adopted by ARC 7793C effective 5/22/24) lists 17 provisions every § 285.5 contract must contain, including: contractor furnishes/operates a legally approved vehicle (43.21(1)); compliance with uniform standards (43.21(2)–(4)); mechanical condition and inspection (43.21(5)–(6)); board-approved drivers only with current school-bus-driver authorization (43.21(7)); medical exam certificate (43.21(8)); driver in-service instruction (43.21(9)); insurance with policy on file with superintendent (43.21(10)); reports to DOE/AEA/superintendent (43.21(11)); restricted use to enrolled students and approved extracurriculars (43.21(12)); reporting of bus-passing-law violations (43.21(13)); board's right to change routing of the bus with extra-mileage allowance (43.21(14)); sanctions for immoral conduct or alcohol use (43.21(15)); the **90-day mutual termination clause** per § 285.5(4) (43.21(16)); and the contractor-to-board **equipment buy-out** on contractor-initiated termination per § 285.5(1) (43.21(17), with an express exception for passenger vehicles used as school buses).
- **No general state-mandated competitive-bidding threshold for school district services.** Iowa Code ch. 26 ("Public Construction Bidding") and § 73A.18 apply only to the "construction, erection, demolition, alteration or repair of a public improvement" (verbatim from § 73A.18), not to operating service contracts. School districts set their own thresholds at which they will solicit competitive quotations or bids for services such as transportation; representative templates (Iowa School Finance Information Services 705.1 / 705.01, used by districts including Iowa City CSD and South Tama County CSD) reflect this discretion. Although nothing requires a rebid at the end of a § 285.5 term, districts commonly run RFPs at term-end as a matter of board policy and good practice, not state-law command.
- **Equipment buy-out on contractor termination.** Iowa Code § 285.5(1)(b): if the contractor desires to terminate the contract and the board desires to purchase the equipment, the contractor must sell at a price set by a three-member appraisal board (one chosen by the board, one by the contractor, third selected by those two). IAC 281—43.21(17) repeats this requirement but expressly exempts passenger vehicles used as school buses.
- **90-day termination right is absolute and mutual.** § 285.5(4) makes the termination right available to either party; IAC 281—43.21(16) makes it a mandatory contract clause; the IDOE form clarifies that termination may not be effective mid-semester absent both parties' agreement.
- **Mandatory contractor-outside-board rule.** § 285.5(7) requires transportation service contracts and driver contracts for school-owned buses to be "made with someone outside the board" except where no other service is available (in which case a board member may transport their own children).
- **Insurance, vehicle, and driver requirements not optional.** § 285.5(6) requires liability insurance "in amounts and kind as provided in the official contract"; § 285.5(3) requires every contractor vehicle to be inspected, approved, and certified before operation; IAC 281—43.7 requires contractor vehicles to meet school-bus construction/equipment standards; IAC 281—43.21(7) requires every contractor driver to hold a current school-bus-driver authorization.
- **Cooperative purchasing / piggybacking.** Iowa Code ch. 28E ("Joint Exercise of Governmental Powers") authorizes interlocal agreements among public agencies, including school districts and AEAs. Districts may cooperate to procure transportation services or to share an AEA-operated arrangement for special-education transportation under § 256B.4(1)(e). The § 285.5 form and three-year cap still govern any private-contractor leg of such an arrangement.
- **Sole-source / emergency procurement.** Iowa Code ch. 285 and IAC 281—ch. 43 contain no sole-source or emergency-procurement provisions specific to transportation. Because there is no state-mandated competitive bidding for transportation services, no formal "sole-source exemption" is needed; a board may award without competition unless its own policy requires otherwise.
- **Charter school rules.** Iowa Code ch. 256E governs charter schools; transportation contracting by charters is generally addressed in the charter contract.
- **Common-carrier transportation.** Iowa Code § 285.1(5) allows districts to use common carriers under uniform DOE standards when school-bus transportation is impracticable; this is a separate authorization track, not a § 285.5 contract.
- **Pricing limits for nonpublic-pupil transportation contracts.** Iowa Code § 285.1(17)(b) provides that when a district contracts with a private party to satisfy nonpublic-pupil transportation duties under § 285.5, the contract "shall not provide payment in excess of the average per pupil transportation costs of the school district for that year." This is a price ceiling, not a procurement-method requirement.
- **No statutory CPI/index, fuel escalator, or evergreen renewal mechanism.** Such mechanisms, if used, must be written into the negotiated terms of a § 285.5 contract and re-executed (within the 3-year cap) at term-end as a new contract.
exceptionsUnresolved: All five fields resolved on primary authority after adversarial validation. The only finding revised during validation was a citation correction in the Special-Education field (corrected from IAC 281—41.530(5) to IAC 281—41.412); the substantive conclusion (no separate special-education contract-term/rebid/renewal rule) is unchanged.

sourceLedgerRows.citation: Iowa Code § 285.5 (2026), 'Contracts for transportation' (Iowa Code Chapter 285, 'State Aid for Transportation')
sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: Iowa Code 2026 edition; PDF footer 'Thu Dec 11 19:29:51 2025 Iowa Code 2026, Section 285.5 (22, 0)'; amendment history line '85 Acts, ch 212, §21; 2010 Acts, ch 1061, §180; 2011 Acts, ch 25, §24'; verified current as of 2026-06-23
sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Max Contract Term (3 years); Rebid Required (silent — i.e., no statutory requirement); Renewal Mechanics (silent — i.e., re-contract within cap); 90-day termination right; mandatory written form; equipment buy-out on contractor termination; mandatory DOE uniform form (subsection 5); insurance, board-outside-the-board rule, driver/vehicle requirements
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Subsection (1)(a) verbatim: 'Contracts for school bus service with private parties shall be in writing ... Contracts may be made for a period not to exceed three years.' Subsection (1)(b): contractor-to-board equipment sale on contractor termination, price by three-member appraisal board. Subsection (4) verbatim: 'All contracts may be terminated by either party on a ninety-day notice.' Subsection (5) verbatim: 'The director of the department of education shall prepare a uniform contract containing provisions not in conflict with this chapter which shall be used by all schools in contracting for transportation service.' Subsection (7): contracts to be 'made with someone outside the board' except where no other transportation service is available.

sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: § 285.10(3) verbatim: '[The local board may] Purchase or lease buses and other transportation facilities, and maintain same, and to enter into contracts for transportation subject to any provisions of law affecting same.' § 285.1(17)(b) verbatim: 'Contracting with private parties as provided in section 285.5. However, contracts shall not provide payment in excess of the average per pupil transportation costs of the school district for that year.' § 285.11(4) verbatim: 'Special contracts for transportation of pupils entitled to transportation shall be entered into only when it is more economical to make such special provision than to provide same by regular bus route, or when by reason of physical or mental disability of the pupil such pupil cannot be transported with safety by bus.'

sourceLedgerRows.citation: Iowa Administrative Code 281—Chapter 43, 'Pupil Transportation' (Division II 'Private Contractors,' Rules 281—43.3 through 281—43.7; Division VII Rule 281—43.21 'Contract — privately owned buses')

sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Renewal Mechanics (17 mandatory contract content provisions); 90-day termination clause as mandatory contract content (43.21(16)); equipment buy-out provision as mandatory contract content (43.21(17), exempting passenger vehicles); contract necessity for all private contractors (43.3); board's right to change routing with extra-mileage allowance (43.21(14)); inspection, driver authorization, and insurance requirements
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Rule 281—43.3 verbatim ('Contract necessary'): 'All private contractors wishing to transport pupils to and from school in privately owned vehicles are to be under contract with the local board of education. This rule does not apply to individuals who transport their own children or other children on a not-for-hire basis.' Rule 281—43.21 enumerates 17 mandatory provisions; subrule 43.21(16) verbatim: 'To allow the contract to be terminated on 90-day notice by either party as provided by Iowa Code section 285.5(4).' Subrule 43.21(17): equipment buy-out per § 285.5(1), with passenger-vehicle exemption.

sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: 'Transportation of eligible individuals shall generally be provided as for other individuals, when appropriate.' Transportation includes travel to/from and between schools and within school buildings; specialized equipment. All transportation equipment must conform to standards in 281—chs. 43 and 44. Rule contains no separate procurement framework for special-education private-contractor agreements.

sourceLedgerRows.sourceTier: 5 - Iowa Association of School Boards 2024 legislative summary (used as variance/implementation reference)
sourceLedgerRows.citation: Iowa Association of School Boards, 'Governance — 2024 Legislative Summary'
sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: Summary of bills enacted in the 2024 Iowa General Assembly
sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Confirms no 2024 amendment to § 285.5; transportation-adjacent 2024 enactment HF 2278 (open-enrollment transportation) does not alter § 285.5 contract-term rules
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Discussion of HF 2278 and other governance bills; no bill amending § 285.5 listed.

sourceLedgerRows.sourceTier: 1 - Codified state statute (general procurement, considered for applicability — materially explains why no service-procurement bid mandate applies)
sourceLedgerRows.citation: Iowa Code § 73A.18 (2026), 'When bids required — advertisement — deposit'
sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: Iowa Code 2026; PDF footer 'Wed Dec 10 22:28:52 2025 Iowa Code 2026, Section 73A.18 (20, 0)'
sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Rebid Required (negative — explains why no state-mandated competitive process applies to operating transportation service contracts)
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Verbatim: 'When the estimated total cost of construction, erection, demolition, alteration or repair of a public improvement exceeds the competitive bid threshold in section 26.3, or as established in section 314.1B, the municipality shall advertise for bids ...' Bidding obligation is keyed to 'public improvement' construction work, not operating services.

challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Some Iowa statute or rule authorizes a transportation contract longer than 3 years (multi-year option, renewal extension, 'notwithstanding' clause, grandfathered arrangement, AEA-led special-ed exception, charter-school exception).
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Re-opened Iowa Code § 285.5 PDF; re-read all 9 subsections; searched chapter 285 PDF for 'years', 'extension', 'option', 'renew', 'successive', 'notwithstanding'; targeted web search 'Iowa school transportation contract longer than three years exception may renew extension option'; checked § 285.10 (board powers); checked § 256B.4(1)(e) (AEA special-ed) — no such authority anywhere.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed. Single, uniform 3-year cap from § 285.5(1)(a); no statutory exception located. § 285.11(4)'s 'special contracts' authority does not carry a different term limit; it merely authorizes individualized arrangements that, if entered with a private contractor, still flow through § 285.5.
challengeMatrixRows.confidenceImpact: Finding holds; primary citation re-verified on official source; confidence remains High.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Rebid Required at Term-End — Not required by state statute
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: An Iowa statute mandates competitive procurement for school district transportation services (e.g., ch. 26 public bidding, § 73A.18, a school-district-services threshold, a procurement chapter applicable to service contracts).
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Re-opened § 73A.18 official PDF: text plainly limits bidding mandate to 'construction, erection, demolition, alteration or repair of a public improvement.' Searched 'Iowa school district competitive bidding required services transportation contract statute'; reviewed Ahlers Cooney client alerts on 2023/2025 bid thresholds; reviewed AIA Iowa Public Owners' Guide; reviewed two district policies. No service-procurement mandate located. § 285.10(3)'s 'subject to any provisions of law affecting same' refers only to § 285.5 (which sets no bidding requirement).
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed. No state statute requires a school district to competitively rebid a transportation services contract at term-end. Cooperative purchasing under ch. 28E is permissive; sole-source/emergency carve-outs are unnecessary because no underlying bidding mandate exists for services.
challengeMatrixRows.confidenceImpact: Finding holds; primary citations re-verified; confidence remains High.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Renewal Mechanics — No statutory renewal mechanism (new written contract required at expiration, subject to same 3-year cap)
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: A statute or rule authorizes automatic renewal, evergreen continuation, statutory CPI escalator, statutory option-year extension, agency-approval renewal, or board-approval extension beyond the 3-year cap.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Searched § 285.5 and IAC 281 ch. 43 for 'renew', 'extension', 'option', 'rollover', 'evergreen', 'CPI', 'fuel adjustment', 'agency approval', 'board approval'; searched chapter 285 PDF (633 lines) for any cross-reference creating an exception; reviewed IDOE Form TR-F-4-497 — no renewal mechanic written into the form. The only in-term price-adjustment language is at IAC 281—43.21(14) (board routing change with mileage cost allowance) and the buy-out at 43.21(17) — neither extends the contract term.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed. No statutory renewal mechanism exists; each successive instrument is a new § 285.5 contract subject to the same 3-year cap. Any escalator/index/fuel-adjustment mechanism must be negotiated within the contract.

challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: The cited statute is mis-numbered, has been renumbered, repealed, or superseded since the code-currency date.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Re-opened official PDF at https://www.legis.iowa.gov/docs/code/285.5.pdf — footer 'Iowa Code 2026, Section 285.5'; amendment history line '85 Acts, ch 212, §21; 2010 Acts, ch 1061, §180; 2011 Acts, ch 25, §24'; checked 2024 IASB legislative summary and 2024/2025 session-law searches for any § 285.5 amendment — none found. Verified that the 2023 Acts ch. 19, § 1052 amendment cited in the chapter applies to § 285.1, not § 285.5.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed. § 285.5 is current, not renumbered, not repealed; amendment trail terminates at 2011.
challengeMatrixRows.confidenceImpact: Finding holds; primary citation re-verified verbatim; confidence remains High.

challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Iowa Code ch. 256B (Special Education) or IAC 281 ch. 41 sets a separate contract-term ceiling, rebid trigger, or renewal mechanic for special-education student transportation procurement.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Searched chapter 256B and IAC 281 ch. 41 for separate procurement rules; searched 'Iowa special education transportation contract separate procurement statute'; re-opened IDOE Chapter 14 manual. Identified that the correct special-ed transportation rule is IAC 281—41.412 (not Stage 1's '281—41.530(5)'). Re-opened 281—41.412 on Cornell LII: it sets substantive transportation requirements (eligibility, equipment standards conforming to IAC 281 chs. 43 and 44, AEA/LEA cost allocation, IEP specifications) but contains NO procurement language — no contract-term ceiling, no rebid trigger, no renewal mechanic for vendor agreements.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Refuted in part (citation only — corrected from 281—41.530(5) to 281—41.412); substantive finding (no separate special-ed contracting rule) is Confirmed.
challengeMatrixRows.confidenceImpact: Substantive finding holds. Citation corrected in the reconciled pack. Confidence remains High.

challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Districts interpret the 3-year cap or 90-day termination right differently, or have policies that displace § 285.5.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Re-opened two representative district policies during validation: Iowa City CSD policy 705.1 (large urban district) and South Tama County CSD policy 705.01 (rural district, served by Iowa School Finance Information Services template). Both cite Iowa Code §§ 26, 28E, 72.3, 73, 73A, 285, 297, 301 as legal references but contain NO provision specific to transportation contract term or renewal that displaces or reinterprets § 285.5. Variance is confined to local-discretionary matters (e.g., $5,000/$50,000 service-procurement quotation thresholds set by board policy).
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed. No district policy located displaces or reinterprets § 285.5's 3-year cap or 90-day termination right.

challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: § 285.5 was amended, repealed, renumbered, or superseded after the code-currency date; IAC 281—43.21 was amended after the cited ARC.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Re-opened § 285.5 official PDF (Iowa Code 2026 footer); checked 2024 IASB session summary; checked 2024-2025 ARC filings for chapter 43 — confirmed ARC 7793C (eff. 5/22/24) and ARC 8240C (eff. 11/6/24, amending rule 43.13 only); chapter 43 PDF footer dated IAC 2/5/25.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed current. § 285.5 last amended 2011 (technical only). IAC 281—43.21 substantively in effect under ARC 7793C since 5/22/24, unchanged.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Applicability boundary — school-specific vs. general procurement
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: A general procurement statute (Iowa Code ch. 26 or § 73A.18) reaches school-district transportation services despite Stage 1's conclusion.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Re-opened § 73A.18 official PDF verbatim — bidding mandate keyed to 'construction, erection, demolition, alteration or repair of a public improvement', expressly limited. Ch. 26 similarly captures only 'public improvement' construction. § 285.10(3)'s 'subject to any provisions of law affecting same' modifier points only to § 285.5 (which sets no bidding requirement). No interpretive authority (AG opinion, IDOE manual) extends ch. 26/§ 73A.18 to operating transportation services.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed. § 285.5 is the only applicable contracting statute for school-district private transportation services.

perFieldVerdicts.note: Iowa Code § 285.5(1)(a) verbatim, re-opened on official Iowa Legislature PDF: 'Contracts may be made for a period not to exceed three years.' IDOE Form TR-F-4-497 reproduces '[Period not to exceed 3 years.]'. No statutory or regulatory exception located.

No Iowa statute requires school-district rebidding of a transportation services contract at term-end. § 73A.18 verbatim re-opened: bidding mandate limited to 'construction, erection, demolition, alteration or repair of a public improvement.' § 285.5 silent on procurement method; § 285.10(3) defers to § 285.5.

Neither § 285.5 nor IAC 281 ch. 43 establishes statutory renewal, evergreen, CPI, fuel escalator, or option-year extension. Each successive instrument is a new § 285.5 contract subject to the same 3-year cap. In-term route/cost adjustment available under IAC 281—43.21(14).

Iowa Code § 285.5 (2026) re-verified verbatim on official Iowa Legislature PDF; amendment history line confirms last substantive amendment in 2011 (technical). IAC 281—43.21 re-verified verbatim under ARC 7793C, IAB 4/17/24, eff. 5/22/24. ARC 7590C notice re-verified.

Re-verified two representative district policies (Iowa City CSD 705.1, South Tama County CSD 705.01). Neither displaces or reinterprets § 285.5. Variance confined to local-discretionary matters (service-procurement quotation thresholds, in-term price-adjustment language) within a uniform statutory ceiling.

Citation only refuted: Stage 1 cited IAC 281—41.530(5); the correct special-education transportation rule is IAC 281—41.412 (re-verified during validation). The substantive finding — no separate contract-term/rebid/renewal rule for special-education student transportation — is unchanged and remains confirmed. Corrected cite incorporated in reconciled pack.

verificationNotes: **SOURCE-VALIDATION REVISIT (load-bearing primary citations).** (1) Iowa Code § 285.5 (2026) — re-opened official Iowa Legislature PDF at https://www.legis.iowa.gov/docs/code/285.5.pdf via firecrawl/WebFetch (downloaded PDF; extracted via pdftotext for verbatim quotation). Footer 'Thu Dec 11 19:29:51 2025 Iowa Code 2026, Section 285.5'. Subsection (1)(a) verbatim: 'Contracts may be made for a period not to exceed three years.' Subsection (4) verbatim: 'All contracts may be terminated by either party on a ninety-day notice.' Subsection (5) verbatim: 'The director of the department of education shall prepare a uniform contract ... which shall be used by all schools in contracting for transportation service.' Amendment history line: '85 Acts, ch 212, §21; 2010 Acts, ch 1061, §180; 2011 Acts, ch 25, §24'. Status: VERIFIED. (2) Iowa Code Chapter 285 PDF re-opened; § 285.10(3), § 285.1(17)(b), § 285.11(4) verbatim confirmed. Status: VERIFIED. (3) IAC 281—43.21 — re-opened single-rule PDF at https://www.legis.iowa.gov/docs/ACO/rule/281.43.21.pdf; all 17 subrules verbatim. Subrule 43.21(16) verbatim: 'To allow the contract to be terminated on 90-day notice by either party as provided by Iowa Code section 285.5(4).' Subrule 43.21(17) verbatim equipment-buy-out with passenger-vehicle exemption. Footer '[ARC 7793C, IAB 4/17/24, effective 5/22/24]'. Status: VERIFIED. (4) IAC 281 ch. 43 full chapter PDF re-opened at https://www.legis.iowa.gov/docs/iac/chapter/281.43.pdf; rule 281—43.3 verbatim ('Contract necessary'). Status: VERIFIED. (5) IDOE Form TR-F-4-497 re-opened; '[Period not to exceed 3 years.]' and '90-day written termination notice' verbatim. Status: VERIFIED. (6) ARC 7590C re-opened official PDF; verbatim 'The State Board of Education hereby proposes to rescind Chapter 43 ... and to adopt a new chapter with the same title.' Status: VERIFIED. (7) Iowa Code § 73A.18 re-opened official PDF; verbatim 'When the estimated total cost of construction, erection, demolition, alteration or repair of a public improvement exceeds the competitive bid threshold ...' — confirms ch. 26/§ 73A.18 are inapplicable to operating service contracts. Status: VERIFIED. (8) IDOE Chapter 14 manual re-opened; § 285.5 reproduced verbatim including 3-year cap and 90-day termination. Status: VERIFIED. (9) IAC 281—41.412 re-opened on Cornell LII — Stage 1 mis-cited this rule as 281—41.530(5); corrected cite is 281—41.412 'Transportation', which sets substantive transportation-as-related-service requirements but no separate procurement framework. Status: VERIFIED with citation correction. (10) District policies re-verified: Iowa City CSD 705.1 and South Tama County CSD 705.01 — neither displaces or reinterprets § 285.5. Status: VERIFIED. **Adversarial challenge results.** Eight challenge rows recorded (5 required fields + currentness + applicability-boundary + a duplicate sub-challenge embedded in the Special-Education row). Outcome: 5 fields confirmed; 1 field (Special Education) citation refuted-and-corrected (cite changed from IAC 281—41.530(5) to IAC 281—41.412) but underlying substantive finding unchanged. No load-bearing primary citation refuted. No re-research pass triggered beyond the citation correction itself. **Final confidence: High** — all five fields directly answered by re-opened primary text; current-law check documented; no unresolved authoritative conflict remains.
